reasoning: Q3 iteration 1 improved but execution time (429ms) is 16% above baseline (370ms) and 4.3x slower than DuckDB (99ms). With only 1 iteration remaining, targeted optimization of the highest-impact bottlenecks is critical. Recommendations 1-3 (hash table replacement, hash function quality, Bloom filter tuning) are low-risk and can deliver 35-50% cumulative improvement, bringing performance to 220-280ms range.
focus_areas: Replace std::unordered_map with compact hash table (Robin Hood or Abseil SwissTable) in GroupKey aggregation map and order lookup map
Improve GroupKey hash function from XOR-based to XXH3/MurmurHash for better distribution and lower collision rates
Optimize Bloom filter: increase bit budget to 8M bits (reduce FP rate from 10% to 1-2%) and use high-quality hash function
strategy_notes: Prioritize recommendations in order: 1) compact hash table (highest ROI, 35-50% improvement expected), 2) hash function (10-20% improvement), 3) Bloom filter (5-10% improvement). These three alone can deliver ~55-80% total speedup. Defer recommendation 4 (merge partitioning) and 5 (SIMD) to conserve iteration budget—focus on proven, low-risk optimizations. Target: 250-280ms execution time.
notes: All three recommendations use well-established techniques documented in knowledge base (compact-hash-tables.md, external-libs/abseil-folly.md, indexing/bloom-filters.md). Compile with -O2 -std=c++17 as before. No correctness concerns—query already produces correct results.
